element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Available immediately as combo attack option
Dropped by defeated enemies; can be preserved or used immediately
NES version introduces hand grenades as countermeasure specifically for this boss
Dual-purpose: fire for damage or gun whip to conserve ammo
Used against helicopter and tank bosses

element_introduction_order.element: New enemy types (frogmen, shotgun users, fat strongman)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: NES-exclusive enemies with distinct attack patterns
Contained in huts; require clearing interior enemies for health recovery, brass knuckles, or armor
Final vehicle encounter before confronting enemy general
Ultimate combat challenge before escape sequence

typical_relief_triggers: Finding power-up hut with health recovery
typical_tension_triggers: Entering new stage with unfamiliar enemy types
Being surrounded by multiple armed enemies
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Wave-based escalation with major tension peaks at end-of-stage bosses; relief provided by power-up opportunities between waves; difficulty ramping through progressive enemy type introduction and vehicle encounters

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Huts and rooms containing power-ups are discoverable side areas requiring player to enter and clear enemies

difficulty_curve.early_game: Stage 1 (POW camp): Basic soldier engagement with helicopter boss; introduces core combat and grenade mechanic
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Stages 2-3 (Warehouse and Jungle): New enemy types (frogmen, shotgun users, strongman) increase challenge; power-up huts provide strategic resource management; machine gun introduces ranged options
difficulty_curve.late_game: Stage 4 (Enemy base): Tank boss encounter; final general boss fight; culminates in escape sequence; highest concentration of advanced enemy types
notable_spikes: Stage 1 helicopter boss - first major test requiring grenade usage
Stage 4 tank encounter - second vehicle boss with increased durability
Stage 4 general boss - final combat encounter with likely highest health/aggression

notable_level_patterns.description: Each stage follows progression: open combat arena → scattered power-up huts/rooms → stage-end boss encounter. Forces player to explore for resources before facing peak difficulty.
Major stages conclude with vehicle encounters (helicopter Stage 1, tank Stage 4) rather than humanoid bosses, creating distinct pacing and requiring different tactical approaches
New enemy archetypes (frogmen, shotgun users, fat strongman) appear in middle stages, not overwhelming early game but escalating throughout progression

sources_used: Wikipedia - P.O.W.: Prisoners of War (gameplay section, NES version differences)
The Cutting Room Floor - P.O.W.: Prisoners of War (NES) (technical notes on level select)
